content.full_text:
CITY OF LEMON GROVE
OFFICE OF THE CITY COUNCIL

July 27, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Ste 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812
ATTN: BLANCA BREEZE

RE: LETTER NO: 92-401

Dear Ms. Breeze:

Thank you for your recent telephone call wherein you informed me that given the parameters of my original inquiry, there would be no conflict of interest in my entering into a lease for property within the Lemon Grove redevelopment area. I understand that I will be receiving an advisory letter within four to six weeks which will confirm this.

Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation and particularly your taking the time to respond in such a quick fashion.

Yours very truly,

BRIAN COCHRAN
Mayor
City of Lemon Grove

BC/lad
CC: Don McLean
    City Attorney

3232 Main Street Lemon Grove, California 92045 Telephone (619) 464-6934

CITY OF LEMON GROVE
OFFICE OF THE CITY COUNCIL

July 10, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Ste. 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95805

Dear Sir/Madam:

I am writing to request a written opinion from you concerning
the relocation of my law office into the redevelopment area of the
city of Lemon Grove.

I have been a Lemon Grove City Councilman since September 26,
1988 and have voted on various redevelopment issues which have come
to the council redevelopment agency's attention for the past four
years. One of the projects in the redevelopment area has been a
property upgrade utilizing agency money, which I have voted to pay
through the agency. Currently, in the redevelopment zone in one of
the parcels of property, there is space available which is well
suited for my law office. I have been thinking about relocating my
office to Lemon Grove for some time and I would like to enter into
a landlord/tenant relationship in this property. This would be
strictly a lease agreement without any options to purchase or any
rights of ownership whatsoever in the parcel of property. My
understanding of the law is that this is not a problem and there
should be no conflict but I want to make sure that this is clear on
the record so that there is no question later that there might be
a conflict of interest.

You should additionally be aware that my voting on these
issues had nothing to do with the possibility of renting this
property since I had no idea the property would come up for rent in
the future and my vote will not result in any diminished rent but,
in fact, the property now is probably renting for more than it had
in the past.

3232 Main Street Lemon Grove, California 92045 Telephone (619) 464-6934

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 10, 1992
Page two


     Since I do not wish to lose this opportunity, your earliest
response to this letter would be most appreciated.

                                        Yours very truly,

                                        [signature]
                                        BRIAN COCHRAN
                                        Mayor
                                        City of Lemon Grove


BC/lad
cc:  Don McLean
     City Attorney
     3232 Main Street
     Lemon Grove, CA 91945
